label: chapter=1, username=liang12e
title: Chapter Chapter 1 Introduction to Computers, Programs, and Java

questions.text: ________ is the physical aspect of the computer that can be seen.
questions.explanation: Hardware refers to the tangible, physical components of a computer system, such as the monitor, keyboard, and internal circuitry, that can be seen and touched.

questions.text: __________ is the brain of a computer.
questions.explanation: The Central Processing Unit (CPU) is known as the brain of the computer because it processes instructions and manages the flow of information through the system.

questions.text: The speed of the CPU may be measured in __________. Please select all that apply.
questions.explanation: CPU speed, also known as clock speed, is measured in cycles per second using the unit Hertz; megahertz (millions of cycles) and gigahertz (billions of cycles) are the common measurements for modern processors.

questions.text: Why do computers use zeros and ones?

choices.label: A
choices.text: because combinations of zeros and ones can represent any numbers and characters.

choices.label: B
choices.text: because digital devices have two stable states and it is natural to use one state for 0 and the other for 1.

choices.label: D
choices.text: because binary numbers are the bases upon which all other number systems are built.

questions.explanation: Computers operate on binary because digital electronic circuits are made of transistors that act as switches, naturally having two stable states: 'on' (represented as 1) and 'off' (represented as 0).

questions.text: One byte has ________ bits.
questions.explanation: In computing, a byte is the standard unit of information consisting of a sequence of 8 bits, which is sufficient to store a single character of text.

questions.text: One gigabyte is approximately ________ bytes.
questions.explanation: Using the standard metric prefix system, 'giga' signifies one billion (10 to the power of 9), making a gigabyte approximately equal to one billion individual bytes of data.

questions.text: A program and its data must be moved into the computer's _______ before they can be executed by the CPU.
questions.explanation: The CPU can only directly access data stored in primary memory (RAM); therefore, programs and data must be loaded from slower storage devices into memory before the CPU can execute them.

questions.text: A computer's _______ is volatile; that is, any information stored in it is lost when the system's power is turned off.
questions.explanation: Random Access Memory (RAM) is considered volatile because it depends on an active electrical current to hold data; once the power is cut, all information stored in that memory is lost.

questions.text: Which of the following are storage devices? Please select all that apply.
questions.explanation: Storage devices are hardware used for recording and retrieving data; portable disks, hard disks, flash drives, and CD-ROMs are all examples of media designed to hold data permanently or semi-permanently.

questions.text: The ____________ specifies the number of pixels in horizontal and vertical dimensions of the display device.
questions.explanation: Screen resolution is a measurement of the display's detail, expressed as the total number of pixels arranged horizontally and vertically across the screen.

questions.text: ____________ is a device to connect a computer to a local area network (LAN).
questions.explanation: A Network Interface Card (NIC) is the hardware component required to connect a computer to a local area network (LAN), enabling communication between devices.

questions.text: ____________ are instructions to the computer. Please select all that apply.
questions.explanation: Software and programs are both terms used to describe the collections of instructions that tell computer hardware how to perform specific tasks.

questions.text: Computer can execute the code in ____________.
questions.explanation: Machine language is the lowest-level programming language, consisting of binary code (0s and 1s) that the computer's CPU can understand and execute directly.

questions.text: ___________ translates high-level language program into machine language program.
questions.explanation: A compiler is a specialized program that translates high-level source code into machine language, allowing the computer's processor to execute the instructions.

questions.text: ____________ is an operating system.
questions.explanation: Windows is a widely used operating system developed by Microsoft; the other options provided (Java, C++, Visual Basic, and Ada) are programming languages.

questions.text: _____________ is a program that runs on a computer to manage and control a computer's activities.
questions.explanation: An operating system (OS) is the core software that manages hardware resources, provides common services for software, and controls all activities on a computer.

questions.text: Due to security reasons, Java ___________ cannot run from a Web browser in the new version of Java.
questions.explanation: Java applets were small programs designed to run within web browsers, but they have been phased out in modern versions of Java due to significant security risks.

questions.text: ________ is not an object-oriented programming language.
questions.explanation: C is a procedural programming language, whereas the other options (Java, C++, C#, and Python) are all built on object-oriented programming (OOP) principles.

questions.text: ________ is interpreted.
questions.explanation: Java is considered an interpreted language because its source code is compiled into bytecode, which is then translated into machine instructions by the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) at runtime.

questions.text: ________ is architecture-neutral.
questions.explanation: Java is architecture-neutral because it compiles to platform-independent bytecode, allowing the same program to run on any device that has a compatible Java Virtual Machine (JVM).

questions.text: ________ is a technical definition of the language that includes the syntax and semantics of the Java programming language.
questions.explanation: The Java language specification is a technical document that describes the syntax (grammar) and semantics (meaning) of the Java language to ensure consistency across different implementations.

questions.text: ________ contains predefined classes and interfaces for developing Java programs.
questions.explanation: The Java API (Application Programming Interface) is a massive library of prewritten classes and interfaces provided by the language to perform common tasks like I/O, networking, and data manipulation.

questions.text: ________ consists of a set of separate programs for developing and testing Java programs, each of which is invoked from a command line.
questions.explanation: The Java JDK (Java Development Kit) is a set of command-line tools (like javac and java) used by developers to compile, document, and package Java applications.

questions.text: ________ provides an integrated development environment (IDE) for rapidly developing Java programs. Editing, compiling, building, debugging, and online help are integrated in one graphical user interface.
questions.explanation: An Integrated Development Environment (IDE) provides a single graphical user interface where developers can write, compile, run, and debug code simultaneously, rather than using separate command-line tools.

questions.text: The main method header is written as:
questions.explanation: In Java, the main method must follow a strict signature: 'public static void main(String[] args)'. Note that Java is case-sensitive; 'String' must be capitalized, and 'main' must be lowercase.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is correct?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Every line in a program must end with a semicolon.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Every statement in a program must end with a semicolon.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Every comment line must end with a semicolon.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Every method must end with a semicolon.

choices.label: E
choices.text: Every class must end with a semicolon.

questions.explanation: While blocks (like classes and methods) are enclosed in curly braces, individual Java statements are required by the language syntax to end with a semicolon to indicate the end of an instruction.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is correct to display Welcome to Java on the console?
questions.explanation: Strings in Java must be enclosed in double quotes (""). Choice B is correct because it uses double quotes, whereas single quotes ('') are reserved for individual character literals (char).

questions.text:
What is the output of the following code?

  System.out.println("1 + 2 + 3");
  System.out.println(1 + 2 + 3);
questions.explanation: The first line prints the literal text inside the double quotes as a string, while the second line performs a mathematical addition of the integers 1, 2, and 3 before printing the result (6).

questions.text: The JDK command to just compile a class (not run) in the file Test.java is
questions.explanation: The 'javac' command is the Java compiler. It requires the full filename, including the '.java' extension, to identify the source file that needs to be converted into bytecode.

questions.text: Which JDK command is correct to run a Java application in ByteCode.class?
questions.explanation: The 'java' command is the Java interpreter used to run a program. It expects the name of the class containing the main method, specifically without the '.class' file extension.

questions.text: Java compiler translates Java source code into _________.
questions.explanation: The Java compiler (javac) converts human-readable source code into platform-independent Java bytecode, which is stored in .class files.

questions.text: _________ is a software that interprets Java bytecode.
questions.explanation: The Java Virtual Machine (JVM) is the software component responsible for interpreting and executing Java bytecode on a specific computer platform.

questions.text:
Suppose you define a Java class as follows, the source code should be stored in a file named _________.

public class Test {

}
questions.explanation: Java requires that a file containing a public class must have the same name as that class, followed by the .java extension.

questions.text: The extension name of a Java bytecode file is
questions.explanation: After compilation, the Java compiler produces bytecode files that always carry the .class extension.

questions.text: The extension name of a Java source code file is
questions.explanation: Source code written by a programmer is saved as a text file with the .java extension before it is compiled into bytecode.

questions.text: Which of the following lines is not a Java comment? Please select all that apply.
questions.explanation: Java supports three comment styles: // (single-line), /* */ (multi-line), and /** */ (documentation). The symbols -- and ** are not recognized as comment indicators in Java.

questions.text: Which of the following are the reserved words? Please select all that apply.
questions.explanation: Reserved words, or keywords, are predefined identifiers that have special meanings in Java (e.g., public, static, void, class) and cannot be used for other purposes.

questions.text: Every statement in Java ends with ________.
questions.explanation: In Java, a semicolon (;) acts as a statement terminator, signaling the end of an individual command to the compiler.

questions.text: A block is enclosed inside __________.
questions.explanation: A block of code, which groups multiple statements together (such as in a class or method), is always delimited by opening { and closing } curly braces.

questions.text: Programming style is important, because ______________. Please select all that apply.

choices.label: A
choices.text: a program may not compile if it has a bad style

choices.label: B
choices.text: good programming style can make a program run faster

choices.label: C
choices.text: good programming style makes a program more readable

choices.label: D
choices.text: good programming style helps reduce programming errors

questions.explanation: Good programming style, such as proper indentation and naming, is crucial because it makes code easier to read, maintain, and debug, although it does not affect execution performance or compilation success.

questions.text:
Analyze the following code.

I:
public class Test {
  public static void main(String[] args) {
    System.out.println("Welcome to Java!");
  }
}

II: 
public class Test {  public static void main(String[] args) {    
System.out.println("Welcome to Java!");  } }

choices.label: A
choices.text: Both I and II can compile and run and display Welcome to Java, but the code in II has a better style than I.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only the code in I can compile and run and display Welcome to Java.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only the code in II can compile and run and display Welcome to Java.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Both I and II can compile and run and display Welcome to Java, but the code in I has a better style than II.

questions.explanation: Java is a free-form language, so white space (newlines and indentation) does not affect how the code compiles or executes as long as the tokens are correctly separated. Both versions contain valid syntax, but code snippet I follows standard industry conventions for readability, whereas code snippet II is difficult to read due to the lack of proper line breaks and indentation.

questions.text:
Which of the following code has the best style?

I:
public class Test {
public static void main(String[] args) {
    System.out.println("Welcome to Java!");
  }
}

II:
public class Test {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
    System.out.println("Welcome to Java!");
  }
}

III:
public class Test {
  public static void main(String[] args) {
  System.out.println("Welcome to Java!");
  }
}

IV:
public class Test {
  public static void main(String[] args) {
    System.out.println("Welcome to Java!");
  }
}
questions.explanation: Consistent indentation is a key component of good programming style. In Option IV, each block of code (the class body and the method body) is indented consistently, making the structure of the program clear. Options I, II, and III contain inconsistent or missing indentation for either the method header or the method body.

questions.text: If a program compiles fine, but it produces incorrect result, then the program suffers __________.
questions.explanation: Errors that occur when a program runs but produces an unintended or incorrect result are called logic errors. These differ from syntax (compile) errors, which prevent the program from starting, and runtime errors, which cause the program to crash during execution.

questions.text:
The following code has __________.

public class Test {
  public static void main(String[] args) {
    System.out.println("Welcome to Java!);
  }
}
questions.explanation: The code contains a syntax error because the string literal inside the println method is missing its closing double quote mark. Since the compiler cannot successfully parse the code, it results in a compile error.

questions.text:
The following code has __________.

public class Test {
  public static void main(string[] args) {
    System.out.println("Welcome to Java!");
  }
}
questions.explanation: Java is a case-sensitive language. The standard class used for handling text is 'String', with a capital 'S'. Using 'string' in lowercase causes the compiler to fail because it does not recognize the identifier as a valid type.
